chunks.page_summary: This page provides information on which PLCs and engineering tools are compatible with Rheonics' SRV in-line viscometers and SRD in-line density meters using Ethernet/IP protocol.
chunks.extracted_text:
Phone: +1 713 234 0863

Which PLCs are compatible with Ethernet/IP?
Modified on: Wed, 16 Aug, 2023 at 8:30 PM

Many companies offer Ethernet/IP-compatible controllers and engineering tools, such as Rockwell Automation, Omron, Wago, Schneider Electric, and B&R Automation.

Rheonics' SRV in-line viscometer and SRD in-line density meter can be monitored or controlled using Ethernet/IP compatible engineering tools from the following brands:

1. Rockwell Automation - Rockwell Automation provides a range of engineering tools for Ethernet/IP, including the Studio 5000 Workbench, (https://www.rockwellautomation.com/en-us/products/software/factorytalk/designsuite/studio-5000.html) and Connected Component Workbench, (https://www.rockwellautomation.com/en-us/capabilities/industrial-automation-control/design-and-configuration-software.html) an integrated engineering framework that supports a variety of PLCs, HMIs, and other automation devices. Integration with Studio 5000: Ethernet/IP integration with Allen Bradly PLC using STUDIO 5000 (https://support.rheonics.com/en/support/solutions/folders/81000293570)

[Image of Rockwell Software Studio 5000 interface and Allen-Bradley PLC hardware]
Figure 1. Studio 5000(Left) and PLC programmer(Control Logix) [1]

2. Wago - Wago provides engineering tools for their PFC200 and 750-8212 PLCs, which are PLCs fully compatible with the Ethernet/IP protocol. For integration with Codesys check our support article: Ethernet/IP integration of Rheonics devices using Codesys. (https://rheonics.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/SA/pages/1718583297)
key_facts: Rheonics' SRV in-line viscometer and SRD in-line density meter are compatible with Ethernet/IP engineering tools.
Ethernet/IP-compatible controllers and engineering tools are offered by companies like Rockwell Automation, Omron, Wago, Schneider Electric, and B&R Automation.
Rockwell Automation provides Studio 5000 Workbench and Connected Component Workbench as engineering tools for Ethernet/IP integration.
Studio 5000 is an integrated engineering framework supporting PLCs, HMIs, and other automation devices.
A support article is available for Ethernet/IP integration with Allen Bradley PLC using STUDIO 5000.
Wago offers PFC200 and 750-8212 PLCs that are fully compatible with the Ethernet/IP protocol.
A support article is available for Ethernet/IP integration of Rheonics devices using Codesys for Wago PLCs.
diagram_labels: Figure 1. Studio 5000(Left) and PLC programmer(Control Logix) [1]

Figure 2. Ethernet/IP compatible PLC PFC200 Wago[2]

3. Schneider Electric - SE provides the EcoStruxure Machine Expert Software for setting the controller and the sensors. EcoStruxure Machine Expert offers a seamless and easy-to-use platform to develop and set up machines and industrial systems. Visit the following support article for integration: Ethernet/IP integration with Modicon M262
(https://support.rheonics.com/en/support/solutions/articles/81000404848-ethernet-ip-integration-with-modicon-m262)
key_facts: The image presents a Wago PFC200 PLC, specifically model 750-8215, which supports Ethernet/IP compatibility.
Schneider Electric's EcoStruxure Machine Expert Software is used for configuring the controller and associated sensors.
EcoStruxure Machine Expert provides a seamless and user-friendly platform for developing and configuring industrial machines and systems.
A Rheonics support article details the integration of Ethernet/IP with the Modicon M262 controller.
